source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Management Server Optimizations: Tuning ansible.cfg for performance

options: The number of processes Ansible runs in parallel to reach remote hosts (default 5)
questions.why: forks sets parallelism; the default of 5 is small (many users set 50, 500, or more).

questions.q: What does setting gathering = smart do?
options: Collects facts only when they have not already been collected
questions.why: smart avoids re-gathering facts; paired with a fact cache it boosts performance.

questions.q: Why might you disable host_key_checking (in a controlled environment)?
options: To skip SSH host-key verification and save time at connection
questions.why: Disabling the key check saves connection time -- at a security cost, so only where you control the environment.

questions.q: In ssh_args, what does ControlPersist do?
options: Keeps the SSH connection open so it can be reused on reconnect
questions.why: ControlPersist keeps connections alive for reuse (the docs suggest ~30 minutes), a big time saving.

questions.q: Why set control_path_dir to something short like /tmp/.cp?
options: A too-long connection-socket path can cause problems
questions.why: Shortening the control-path avoids issues when the socket path is too long.

questions.q: What does pipelining = True do, and what must be true first?
options: Reduces the number of SSH connections for running modules; needs requiretty disabled in sudoers
questions.why: pipelining cuts SSH round-trips; the sudoers requiretty option must be disabled first.

questions.q: What is the ansible_managed variable typically used for?
options: A notice inserted into templated files, warning that the file is auto-managed
questions.why: ansible_managed marks deployed template files as managed (changing it can restart daemons via handlers).
